title: secale montanum subsp. anatolicum
intro: Secale montanum subsp. anatolicum, also known as Anatolian rye grass or wild rye, is a subspecies of the rye grass plant that is native to the Anatolian region of Turkey. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Secale montanum subsp. anatolicum include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. It is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties and is also used in some traditional folk remedies.
